questions.question_text: A broker provides brokerage services to a seller under a verbal listing and never puts it in writing. Under NC license law, is the broker's conduct lawful?
questions.correct_answer: No, operating without a written listing is unlawful
distractors: Yes, oral listings are lawful for up to 30 days
Yes, if the seller does not object
Yes, oral listings are fully lawful in NC
questions.explanation: Rule 58A .0104(a) requires every agency agreement between a broker and a property owner to be in writing and signed at the time it is formed, so a broker who provides brokerage services to a seller under an oral listing is violating NC license law and can be disciplined. Separately, G.S. 93A-13 makes an oral broker agreement unenforceable, meaning the broker could not sue to recover a commission on it. The key point for licensees is that NC does not merely treat the oral listing as unenforceable, it prohibits operating without a written listing in the first place.

questions.question_text: A listing agreement does not include an expiration date. What is the consequence under NC rules?
questions.correct_answer: The agreement does not comply with NC law
distractors: It defaults to a 6-month term
It automatically renews each year
It remains valid until either party cancels
questions.explanation: NC requires every listing agreement to contain a definite expiration date. There is no default term or automatic renewal provision under the law. A listing without an expiration date fails to meet the mandatory requirements and does not comply with NC regulations.

questions.question_text: Which required element must be included in every NC listing agreement under Rule 58A .0104(b)?
distractors: A guaranteed minimum sale price
The broker's social security number
A mandatory arbitration clause
questions.explanation: Rule 58A .0104(b) requires all listing agreements to contain anti-discriminatory language, reinforcing Fair Housing compliance. There is no requirement for guaranteed prices, social security numbers, or mandatory arbitration clauses. The listing must also include the broker's individual license number.

questions.question_text: A broker prepares a listing agreement but omits her individual license number. Has she complied with NC requirements?
questions.correct_answer: No, the individual license number is required
distractors: Yes, the firm license number is sufficient
Yes, if the BIC's number is included
Only the MLS number is required
questions.explanation: NC listing agreements must include the listing broker's individual license number. The firm license number or BIC's number alone is not sufficient. This requirement ensures accountability and allows consumers and the Commission to identify the specific broker responsible for the listing.

questions.question_text: A listing agreement is signed by the broker and only one of two property co-owners. Is this agreement valid?
questions.correct_answer: No, all parties must sign the agreement
distractors: Yes, one owner can bind the property
Yes, if the signing owner has majority share
Only if the other owner consents verbally
questions.explanation: NC requires listing agreements to be signed by all parties. When a property has co-owners, each owner must sign the listing agreement for it to be valid. One co-owner cannot unilaterally bind the other, and verbal consent from the non-signing owner is insufficient.

questions.question_text: A broker has been showing properties to a buyer under a verbal buyer agency agreement for two weeks. The buyer wants to make an offer. What must happen first?
questions.correct_answer: The agreement must be reduced to writing
distractors: The buyer must obtain preapproval
The broker must notify the listing agent
Nothing, verbal agreements cover offers
questions.explanation: A temporary oral buyer agency agreement in NC must be nonexclusive with no specific time period. However, it must be reduced to writing no later than the making of the first offer. The broker cannot submit an offer while still operating under only a verbal buyer agency arrangement.

questions.question_text: Under what condition may a buyer agency agreement in NC begin as an oral arrangement?
questions.correct_answer: It must be nonexclusive with no set period
distractors: It must be for fewer than 7 days
It must cover only one property
It requires BIC approval first
questions.explanation: NC permits temporary oral buyer agency only if it is nonexclusive and has no specific time period. If the agreement is exclusive to a particular company or limited to a specific time period, it must be in writing from the start. This exception allows informal initial relationships while protecting consumers.

questions.question_text: A buyer wants an exclusive buyer agency agreement limited to 60 days with one specific firm. When must this agreement be in writing?
distractors: Before the first offer is made
Within 3 days of the verbal agreement
At the time of the first showing
questions.explanation: When a buyer agency agreement is limited to a specific company or a specific time period, it must be in writing immediately. The oral agreement exception applies only to nonexclusive arrangements with no defined period. An exclusive 60-day agreement with one firm requires immediate written documentation.

questions.question_text: Before listing a property, a broker reviews the deed, survey, and HOA documents. What is this process called?
distractors: Due diligence period review
Title examination
Comparative market analysis
questions.explanation: Prelisting activities include reviewing the deed, survey, covenants, HOA information, mortgage details, prior inspections, and preparing a CMA. These steps help the broker understand the property and set appropriate expectations. A CMA is just one component of the broader prelisting process.

questions.question_text: A buyer asks a broker to represent them exclusively for 30 days, limited to properties listed by ABC Realty. The broker agrees verbally. Is this proper?
questions.correct_answer: No, this must be written immediately
distractors: Yes, verbal is fine for 30 days or less
Yes, if limited to one brokerage's listings
Yes, until the buyer makes an offer
questions.explanation: Because this buyer agency is limited to a specific company (ABC Realty) and a specific time period (30 days), it must be in writing from the outset. The oral buyer agency exception only applies to nonexclusive arrangements with no set time period. Both limitations here trigger the immediate writing requirement.

questions.question_text: Does a broker need a separate property management license in North Carolina?
questions.correct_answer: No, a broker license covers property management
distractors: Yes, a separate PM license is required
Only for managing over 10 units
Only for commercial properties
questions.explanation: North Carolina does not require a separate property management license. A broker license is sufficient to engage in property management activities. However, the broker must still comply with all PM-specific rules and maintain written agreements from inception as required by Rule 58A .0104(a).

questions.question_text: A property manager begins managing a rental property with only a handshake agreement from the owner. When must a written PM agreement be in place?
distractors: Within 30 days of the first lease
Before collecting the first rent payment
Within 90 days of starting management
questions.explanation: Under Rule 58A .0104(a), property management agreements must be in writing from inception. There is no grace period or waiting period. Beginning management activities under only a verbal agreement violates NC rules regardless of when rent collection or leasing begins.

questions.question_text: A property manager selects tenants for a rental property. Which federal law must guide the selection process?
distractors: The Truth in Lending Act
The Real Estate Settlement Act
The Americans with Disabilities Act only
questions.explanation: Tenant selection must comply with the Fair Housing Act, which prohibits discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, familial status, and disability. While the ADA also applies to certain aspects of property management, the Fair Housing Act is the primary law governing tenant selection criteria.

questions.question_text: Which of the following must be included in a NC property management agreement?
questions.correct_answer: The management fee and agent authority
distractors: The tenant's credit score minimum
The owner's tax identification number
The projected annual property appreciation
questions.explanation: NC property management agreements must include a property description, contract period, management fee, agent authority, and responsibilities. Tenant credit requirements, owner tax IDs, and appreciation projections are not mandatory elements of the PM agreement itself, though they may be addressed operationally.

questions.question_text: A property manager handles a commercial building with public access areas. Beyond Fair Housing, which additional federal law applies to the property?
distractors: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform Act
Sarbanes-Oxley Compliance Act
Sherman Antitrust Act
questions.explanation: The Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) applies to commercial properties with public access areas, requiring accessibility accommodations. Property managers must ensure ADA compliance for common areas, entrances, and facilities. The other listed laws address financial regulation and antitrust matters, not property accessibility.
